title: cassine rehmannii
intro: Cassine rehmannii, also known as Cassine, is a plant species native to South Africa, belonging to the family Rhamnaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves and bark, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The health benefits associated with Cassine rehmannii include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of Cassine rehmannii can be made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, or used in combination with other herbs to create tinctures and ointments for topical application.
